note: No linkedin_profiles.json from previous runs — all notes are 'generic' type using local business context. Once HANDS executes the search plan and writes data/linkedin_profiles.json, re-run this agent to generate post_reference or headline notes for higher conversion. Character counts verified against 200-char LinkedIn free account limit.

strategy.tone: slightly awkward genuine compliment — stands out from polished AI outreach
anti_patterns: Do not use 'I came across your profile'
Do not use 'I was impressed by'
Do not mention AI or automation
Do not pitch in the note — curiosity only
Do not use 'reach out' or 'touch base'
strategy.upgrade_path: Once profile data available: replace generic with post_reference (highest) or headline type

connection_notes.linkedin_url: TBD — HANDS to populate from search plan
connection_notes.note: Rachel — your garden design work in Skipton caught my attention while I was looking at local creative businesses. Spotted something about the site worth a quick mention. Connecting in case it's useful.
connection_notes.rationale: First name known, local reference, vague 'spotted something' creates curiosity without pitching. 'Caught my attention' is mildly awkward — avoids AI polish.
connection_notes.upgrade_note: Replace with post_reference if Rachel has posted about a garden project or spring gardening content recently

connection_notes.linkedin_url: TBD — HANDS to populate from search plan
connection_notes.note: Found Boroughbridge Plumbing while looking at local trades in North Yorkshire. Something on the website caught my eye — worth a quick mention. Connecting in case it's relevant.
connection_notes.rationale: No owner name — keep impersonal but specific to location and trade. 'Caught my eye' is more human than 'I noticed'. No pitch, just curiosity hook.
connection_notes.condition: Only send if HANDS identifies a named individual — do not connect to a generic company LinkedIn page

connection_notes.linkedin_url: TBD — HANDS to populate from search plan
connection_notes.note: Graham — Ilkley Ironmongery stood out when I was looking at independent hardware businesses in Wharfedale. Something about the site I thought was worth a mention. Connecting in case it's helpful.
connection_notes.rationale: First name known. 'Independent hardware businesses' is a genuine compliment category — independent shops are rare and owners tend to be proud of that. 'Wharfedale' is specific and local, not generic. Day 8 — last chance before email breakup on Apr 11, so this note should be warm but not desperate.
connection_notes.upgrade_note: If Graham posts about the hardware trade, DIY, or local Ilkley content, reference that instead

connection_notes.linkedin_url: TBD — HANDS to populate from search plan
connection_notes.note: Steve — Halifax Window Cleaning came up when I was looking at local trades businesses in Calderdale. Noticed something on the website. Connecting in case it's worth a quick chat.
connection_notes.rationale: First name known. 'Calderdale' is more specific than 'Halifax' alone — shows local awareness. 'Came up' is natural. Day 3 lead — keep note especially light. HANDS: only connect if Steve's profile shows activity in last 30 days.
connection_notes.condition: Day 3 caution — only send if profile is demonstrably active (recent post or engagement)

connection_notes.linkedin_url: TBD — HANDS to populate from search plan
connection_notes.note: Found Pontefract Pharmacy while looking at independent local pharmacies in West Yorkshire. Noticed something about the website that seemed worth flagging. Connecting in case it's useful.
connection_notes.rationale: 'Independent local pharmacies' is a genuine framing — pharmacist-owners identify with that label. No owner name so no first-name opener. Day 3 low-priority lead.
connection_notes.condition: Low priority — only send if HANDS finds a named pharmacist-owner with clear recent LinkedIn activity. Skip if only a company page is found.

connection_notes.linkedin_url: TBD — HANDS to populate from search plan
connection_notes.note: Ann — Castleford Carpets came up while I was looking at local flooring and home businesses in West Yorkshire. Spotted something on the site worth mentioning. Connecting in case it's relevant.
connection_notes.rationale: First name known. 'Flooring and home businesses' broadens the context naturally. 'Spotted something' is the curiosity hook without a pitch. Day 3 — same active-profile condition as Steve.
connection_notes.condition: Day 3 caution — only send if Ann's profile shows recent activity

summary.notes: All notes are generic pending profile data. Run HANDS search plan first, write linkedin_profiles.json, then re-run BRAIN to upgrade notes to post_reference or headline type. Leads 4 and 8 (day 7+) are highest priority — no activity condition needed, just profile confirmation.
